            "lineNumber": 381,
            "text": "# Build for production"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 382,
            "text": "npm run build"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 383
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 384,
            "text": "# Preview production build locally"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 385,
            "text": "npm run preview"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 386
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 387,
            "text": "# Format code with Prettier"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 388,
            "text": "npm run format"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 389,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 390
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 391,
            "text": "### Setup Commands"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 392
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 393,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 394,
            "text": "# Initial project setup (run once)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 395,
            "text": "npm run project-setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 396
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 397,
            "text": "# Update Hugo modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 398,
            "text": "npm run update-modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 399
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 400,
            "text": "# Update theme to latest version"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 401,
            "text": "npm run update-theme"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 402,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 403
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 404,
            "text": "### Utility Commands"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 405
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 406,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 407,
            "text": "# Set up theme structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 408,
            "text": "npm run theme-setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 409
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 410,
            "text": "# Remove dark mode feature"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 411,
            "text": "npm run remove-darkmode"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 412
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 413,
            "text": "# Clear modules cache"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 414,
            "text": "npm run clear-modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 415,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 416
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 417,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 418
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 419,
            "text": "## 🚀 Deployment (Going Live)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 420
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 421,
            "text": "### Multi-Platform Deployment Support"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 422
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 423,
            "text": "This project supports deployment on multiple platforms with optimized configurations:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 424
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 425,
            "text": "#### Option 1: AWS Amplify (Recommended)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 426
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 427,
            "text": "**Configuration**: &#96;amplify.yml&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 428,
            "text": "- **Hugo Version**: 0.141.0"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 429,
            "text": "- **Go Version**: 1.23.3"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 430,
            "text": "- **Build Command**: &#96;npm run project-setup \u0026\u0026 npm run build&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 431,
            "text": "- **Output Directory**: &#96;/public&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 432
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 433,
            "text": "1. **Push to GitHub**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 434,
            "text": "2. **Connect** to AWS Amplify"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 435,
            "text": "3. **Auto-deployment** with the included configuration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 436
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 437,
            "text": "#### Option 2: Netlify (Easiest for Beginners)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 438
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 439,
            "text": "**Configuration**: &#96;netlify.toml&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 440,
            "text": "- **Hugo Version**: 0.146.5"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 441,
            "text": "- **Go Version**: 1.24.2"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 442,
            "text": "- **Build Command**: &#96;yarn project-setup; yarn build&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 443,
            "text": "- **Publish Directory**: &#96;public&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 444
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 445,
            "text": "1. **Build your site**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 446,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 446
          },

          }

          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "- &#96;# Big Heading&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "- &#96;## Smaller Heading&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "- &#96;**bold text**&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 153,
            "text": "- &#96;*italic text*&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 154,
            "text": "- &#96;[link text](https://example.com)&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 155
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 156,
            "text": "I create new posts with:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 157,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 158,
            "text": "hugo new posts/my-post-name.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 159,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 160
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 161,
            "text": "### Testing Everything"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 162
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 163,
            "text": "Before putting it live, you can run:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 164,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 165,
            "text": "hugo server -D"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 166,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 167
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 168,
            "text": "This starts a local server at &#96;http://localhost:1313&#96; so you can see how everything looks. The &#96;-D&#96; flag shows draft posts too."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 169
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 170,
            "text": "## Deploying to the Internet"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 171
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 172,
            "text": "Once you're happy with how your site looks locally, it's time to get it online."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 173
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 174,
            "text": "### GitHub Setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 175
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 176,
            "text": "First, create a new repository on GitHub and push your code:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 177
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 178,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 179,
            "text": "git add ."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "git commit -m \"Initial commit\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "git branch -M main"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 182,
            "text": "git remote add origin https://github.com/yourusername/yoursite.com.git"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 183,
            "text": "git push -u origin main"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 184,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "**Pro tip**: It's easy to forget the remote origin step (happened to me twice!). Git error messages can be pretty cryptic when this happens."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188,
            "text": "### Cloudflare Pages Magic"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 190,
            "text": "With your code on GitHub, setting up Cloudflare Pages is surprisingly simple:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 191
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 192,
            "text": "1. Sign up at pages.cloudflare.com"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 193,
            "text": "2. Connect it to your GitHub account"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 194,
            "text": "3. Select your repository"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 195,
            "text": "4. Set build command to &#96;hugo&#96; and output directory to &#96;public&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 196,
            "text": "5. Add environment variable &#96;HUGO_VERSION&#96; with your version number"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 197
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 198,
            "text": "**Important**: Make sure your Hugo version matches what you have locally, or the build will fail. This can save you from some frustrating deployment attempts!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 199
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 200,
            "text": "Hit deploy and boom - live website in about 2 minutes."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 201
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 202,
            "text": "## Common Problems and Solutions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 203
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 204,
            "text": "Here are some common issues you might run into (and how to fix them):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 205
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 206,
            "text": "### Theme Issues"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 207,
            "text": "**Problem**: Site looks like basic HTML instead of the theme."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 208,
            "text": "**Solution**: Check your theme name in the config file. In my case, I had written &#96;HugoPlate&#96; instead of &#96;hugoplate&#96;. Theme names are case sensitive!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 209
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 210,
            "text": "**Problem**: Theme not loading at all."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 211,
            "text": "**Solution**: Make sure you've actually downloaded the theme files and they're in the right &#96;themes/&#96; folder. Also check that your &#96;hugo.toml&#96; theme name matches the folder name exactly."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 212
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 213,
            "text": "### Build and Deployment Failures"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 214,
            "text": "**Problem**: Cloudflare couldn't build my site."
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 214
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 148
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/how-i-built-joraps-world.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.33635759353637695
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "version: 1"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "frontend:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "  phases:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "    preBuild:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "      commands:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "        - yum install -y curl"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "        - curl -LO \"https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/releases/download/v0.141.0/hugo_extended_0.141.0_Linux-64bit.tar.gz\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "        - tar -xvf hugo_extended_0.141.0_Linux-64bit.tar.gz"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "        - mv hugo /usr/local/bin/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "        - rm hugo_extended_0.141.0_Linux-64bit.tar.gz"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "        - echo \"HUGO 0.141.0 INSTALLED\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "        - curl -LO \"https://dl.google.com/go/go1.23.3.linux-amd64.tar.gz\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "        - tar -C /usr/local -xzf go1.23.3.linux-amd64.tar.gz"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "        - export PATH=$PATH:/usr/local/go/bin"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "        - rm go1.23.3.linux-amd64.tar.gz"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "        - echo \"GO 1.23.3 INSTALLED\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "        - npm install"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "    build:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "      commands:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "        - npm run project-setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "        - npm run build"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "  artifacts:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "    # IMPORTANT - Please verify your build output directory"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "    baseDirectory: /public"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    files:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "      - \"**/*\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "  cache:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    paths:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "      - node_modules/**/*"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 29
          },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 441,
            "text": "- **Go Version**: 1.24.2"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 442,
            "text": "- **Build Command**: &#96;yarn project-setup; yarn build&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 443,
            "text": "- **Publish Directory**: &#96;public&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 444
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 445,
            "text": "1. **Build your site**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 446,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 447,
            "text": "   npm run build"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 448,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 449
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 450,
            "text": "2. **Go to [netlify.com](https://netlify.com)**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 451
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 452,
            "text": "3. **Drag the &#96;public&#96; folder** to the deploy area"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 453
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 454,
            "text": "4. **Your site is live!** 🎉"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 455
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 456,
            "text": "#### Option 3: Vercel (Git-based)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 457
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 458,
            "text": "**Configuration**: &#96;vercel.json&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 459,
            "text": "- **Build Script**: &#96;vercel-build.sh&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 460,
            "text": "- **Static Build**: &#96;@vercel/static-build&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 461,
            "text": "- **Output Directory**: &#96;public&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 462
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 463,
            "text": "1. **Push your code** to GitHub"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 464,
            "text": "2. **Connect** your GitHub repo to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 465,
            "text": "3. **Auto-deployment** happens on every push"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 466
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 467,
            "text": "### Before You Deploy"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 468
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 469,
            "text": "1. **Update your site URL** in &#96;hugo.toml&#96;:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 470,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;toml"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 471,
            "text": "   baseURL = \"https://yoursite.com\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 472,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 473
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 474,
            "text": "2. **Test locally**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 475,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 476,
            "text": "   npm run build"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 477,
            "text": "   npm run preview"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 478,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 479
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 480,
            "text": "3. **Check everything works** at &#96;http://localhost:1313&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 481
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 482,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 483
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 484,
            "text": "## 🔧 Troubleshooting"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 485
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 486,
            "text": "### Common Problems and Solutions"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 487
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 488,
            "text": "#### \"Hugo not found\" or \"Hugo command not found\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 489
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 490,
            "text": "**Problem**: Hugo isn't installed or not in your PATH  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 491,
            "text": "**Solution**: "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 492,
            "text": "1. Install Hugo Extended from https://gohugo.io/installation/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 493,
            "text": "2. Make sure it's the \"extended\" version"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 494,
            "text": "3. Restart your terminal/command prompt"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 495
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 496,
            "text": "#### \"Module not found\" errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 497
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 498,
            "text": "**Problem**: Hugo modules aren't set up correctly  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 499,
            "text": "**Solution**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 500,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 501,
            "text": "npm run update-modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 502,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 503
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 504,
            "text": "#### Styles not loading or site looks broken"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 505
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 506,
            "text": "**Problem**: CSS isn't building correctly  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 507,
            "text": "**Solution**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 508,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 509,
            "text": "rm -rf node_modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 510,
            "text": "npm install"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 511,
            "text": "npm run dev"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 512,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 513
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 514,
            "text": "#### Images not showing"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 515
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 516,
            "text": "**Problem**: Wrong image paths  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 517,
            "text": "**Solution**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 518,
            "text": "- Make sure images are in &#96;assets/images/&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 519,
            "text": "- Use relative paths: &#96;images/my-image.jpg&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 520,
            "text": "- Check file names match exactly (case-sensitive)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 521
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 522,
            "text": "#### Build fails with errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 523
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 524,
            "text": "**Problem**: Various build issues  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 525,
            "text": "**Solution**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 526,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 527,
            "text": "# Nuclear option - start fresh"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 528,
            "text": "rm -rf node_modules"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 529,
            "text": "rm -rf resources"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 530,
            "text": "rm -rf public"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 531,
            "text": "npm install"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 532,
            "text": "npm run project-setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 533,
            "text": "npm run build"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 534,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 535
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 536,
            "text": "### Getting Help"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 537
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 538,
            "text": "1. **Read the error message** - it usually tells you what's wrong"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 539,
            "text": "2. **Check [Hugo documentation](https://gohugo.io/documentation/)**"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 539
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 440
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./README.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3351893424987793
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 286,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟡 Create a Progressive Web App** - Make your site work like a mobile app"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 287,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Users can install your site, use it offline*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 288,
            "text": "  - *Features: App icon, offline mode, push notifications*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 289
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 290,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 291
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 292,
            "text": "## 🛠️ Development \u0026 Deployment"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 293
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 294,
            "text": "*Why this matters: Better development process means fewer bugs.*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 295
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 296,
            "text": "### 🟢 Development Workflow"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 297,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟢 Set up version control** - Track changes to your code"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 298,
            "text": "  - *What it does: You can undo changes, collaborate safely*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 299,
            "text": "  - *Tool: Git with GitHub/GitLab*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 300
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 301,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟡 Add automated testing** - Check if your code works correctly"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 302,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Catches bugs before users see them*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 303,
            "text": "  - *Types: Unit tests, integration tests*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 304
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟢 Create a staging environment** - Test changes before going live"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 306,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Safe place to test new features*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 307,
            "text": "  - *Setup: Copy of your live site for testing*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 308
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 309,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟡 Set up continuous deployment** - Automatically deploy when code changes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 310,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Faster updates, fewer manual errors*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 311,
            "text": "  - *Tools: GitHub Actions, Netlify, Vercel*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 312
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 313,
            "text": "### 🟡 Monitoring \u0026 Maintenance"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 314,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟡 Add error tracking** - Get notified when things break"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 315,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Fix problems before users complain*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 316,
            "text": "  - *Tools: Sentry, LogRocket*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 317
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 318,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟢 Monitor website uptime** - Make sure your site is always available"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 319,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Get alerts when your site goes down*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 320,
            "text": "  - *Tools: UptimeRobot, Pingdom*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 321
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 322,
            "text": "- [ ] **🟡 Set up analytics** - Understand how users use your site"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 323,
            "text": "  - *What it does: Make data-driven improvements*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 324,
            "text": "  - *Tools: Google Analytics, Plausible*"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 325
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 326,
            "text": "---"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 327
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 328,
            "text": "## 🎯 Getting Started - Your First Tasks"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 329
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 330,
            "text": "### Week 1: Quick Wins 🟢"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 331,
            "text": "1. **Add alt text to all images** - Improves accessibility and SEO"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 332,
            "text": "2. **Check mobile experience** - Test on your phone"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 333,
            "text": "3. **Add meta descriptions** - Better search results"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 334,
            "text": "4. **Set up basic analytics** - Start tracking visitors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 335
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 336,
            "text": "### Week 2: Code Quality 🟡"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 337,
            "text": "1. **Add ESLint** - Catch JavaScript errors"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 338,
            "text": "2. **Remove unused CSS** - Smaller file sizes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 339,
            "text": "3. **Add proper headings** - Better structure"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 340,
            "text": "4. **Test keyboard navigation** - Accessibility check"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 341
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 285
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./TODO.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.32761549949645996
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 245,
            "text": "**Solution**: Clear your browser cache, or use &#96;hugo server --disableFastRender&#96; to force full rebuilds."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 246
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 247,
            "text": "## The Daily Workflow"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 248
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 249,
            "text": "When you want to write a new post, the process is pretty straightforward:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 250
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 251,
            "text": "1. Create the post file:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 252,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 253,
            "text": "   hugo new posts/post-name.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 254,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 255,
            "text": "2. Write the content in Markdown"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 256,
            "text": "3. Preview locally:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 257,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 258,
            "text": "   hugo server -D"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 259,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 260,
            "text": "4. When you're happy with it, deploy:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 261,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 262,
            "text": "   git add ."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 263,
            "text": "   git commit -m \"New post about whatever\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 264,
            "text": "   git push"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 265,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 266,
            "text": "5. Site updates automatically in ~2 minutes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 267
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 268,
            "text": "This workflow feels so much smoother than the traditional CMS experience - no logging into admin panels, waiting for pages to load, or fighting with editors."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 269
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 270,
            "text": "## Backup Strategy (Don't Skip This)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 271
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 272,
            "text": "Here's the beautiful thing about this setup - **you get automatic backups without thinking about it**:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 273
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 274,
            "text": "**What's automatically backed up:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 275,
            "text": "- All your content and code lives in GitHub (that's your primary backup)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 276,
            "text": "- Every change you make is version controlled (you can revert anything)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 277,
            "text": "- Cloudflare keeps copies of your built site"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 278
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 279,
            "text": "**What I do for extra safety:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 280,
            "text": "- Occasionally download a zip of my GitHub repo to my computer"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 281,
            "text": "- Keep important images in multiple places (Google Drive + GitHub)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 282,
            "text": "- My markdown files are just text, so they're tiny and easy to backup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 283
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 284,
            "text": "**If disaster strikes:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 285,
            "text": "- Lost computer? Clone your GitHub repo to a new machine"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 286,
            "text": "- Accidentally deleted something? Git history has everything"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 287,
            "text": "- Cloudflare goes down? Your GitHub repo can deploy to Netlify or Vercel in minutes"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 288
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 289,
            "text": "This is way more robust than my old WordPress setup where I constantly worried about database backups."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 290
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 291,
            "text": "## What This Costs Me"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 292
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 293,
            "text": "**Current monthly cost: $0**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 294
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 295,
            "text": "Everything I use is free:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 296,
            "text": "- Hugo: Open source"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 297,
            "text": "- GitHub: Free for public repos (which is fine for a personal site)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 298,
            "text": "- Cloudflare Pages: Free forever for personal use"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 299,
            "text": "- SSL certificate: Included"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 300,
            "text": "- Global CDN: Included"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 301
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 302,
            "text": "**Upgrades I already have:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 303,
            "text": "- Custom domain ($12/year)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 304
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 305,
            "text": "**Setting up a custom domain** is straightforward if you want it:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 306,
            "text": "1. Buy domain from any registrar (Namecheap, GoDaddy, etc.)"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 306
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 244
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./content/english/blog/how-i-built-joraps-world.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.32158029079437256
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "# Sveltia CMS setup (Cloudflare Pages + GitHub)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "This guide covers Sveltia CMS on your Hugo site with GitHub OAuth through Cloudflare Pages Functions. Sveltia is a modern successor to Netlify/Decap CMS and uses the same &#96;config.yml&#96; model; see the [migration guide](https://sveltiacms.app/en/docs/migration/netlify-decap-cms) for details."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "## Prerequisites"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "- Hugo site on Cloudflare Pages"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "- GitHub repository for the site"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "- GitHub account with access to the repository"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "## Step 1: Create a GitHub OAuth app"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "1. Go to GitHub **Settings** → **Developer settings** → **OAuth Apps**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "2. Click **New OAuth App**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "3. Set:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "   - **Application name**: e.g. “JoRap CMS”"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "   - **Homepage URL**: &#96;https://www.jorap.com&#96; (your live URL)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "   - **Authorization callback URL**: &#96;https://www.jorap.com/api/callback&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "4. Register the app and note the **Client ID** and **Client Secret**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "## Step 2: Cloudflare Pages environment variables"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "1. Cloudflare Pages → your project → **Settings** → **Environment variables**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "2. For **Production** and **Preview**, add:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "   - &#96;GITHUB_CLIENT_ID&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "   - &#96;GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "## Step 3: CMS configuration"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "1. Open &#96;static/admin/config.yml&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "2. Confirm the backend matches your repo and domain, for example:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;yaml"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "   backend:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "     name: github"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "     repo: jorap/jorap.com"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "     branch: master"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "     base_url: https://www.jorap.com"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "     auth_endpoint: /api/auth"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "3. Admin UI is loaded from &#96;static/admin/index.html&#96; via the Sveltia CMS bundle (&#96;@sveltia/cms&#96;)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "## Step 4: Deploy and test"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "1. Push changes and wait for the Pages build"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "2. Open &#96;https://www.jorap.com/admin/&#96; and sign in with GitHub"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49,
            "text": "## File layout"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "├── static/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "│   └── admin/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "│       ├── index.html    # loads Sveltia CMS"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "│       └── config.yml    # collections \u0026 backend"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "├── functions/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "│   └── api/"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "│       ├── auth.js"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "│       └── callback.js"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "└── SVELTIA_CMS_SETUP.md"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 63
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 64,
            "text": "## Troubleshooting"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 65
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 66,
            "text": "- **Login fails**: Check OAuth callback URL, env vars, and the **Functions** tab for &#96;api/auth&#96; and &#96;api/callback&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 67,
            "text": "- **Cannot save**: Confirm repo name/branch in &#96;config.yml&#96; and that your GitHub user can push to the repo"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 67
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./SVELTIA_CMS_SETUP.md",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3180738091468811
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "[build]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "publish = \"public\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "command = \"yarn project-setup; yarn build\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "[build.environment]"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "HUGO_VERSION = \"0.146.5\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "GO_VERSION = \"1.24.2\""
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 7
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./netlify.toml",
        "signatures": {}
      },
      "score": 0.3172607421875
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "**Learning curve**: If you're coming from a development background, Hugo feels pretty natural to pick up. The templating system makes sense once you get the hang of it, and the documentation is helpful. Setting it up on Windows took about 30 minutes including theme configuration - your experience may vary!"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "**Important note**: Keep track of your Hugo version from the start. You'll need it later when setting up deployment, and version mismatches will cause build failures (trust me on this one!)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "### Cloudflare Pages - Free Hosting That Doesn't Suck"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "This is where the magic happens. Cloudflare Pages hosts static sites for free. Not \"free for 3 months then we charge you\" - genuinely free. I've been using it for months now and haven't paid a cent."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "The best part? It's connected to GitHub, so whenever I push changes to my code, the site updates automatically. No FTP, no manual uploads, nothing. Just push and it's live."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "### GitHub - Where My Code Lives"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "I use GitHub to store all my website files. The Git workflow is standard - commit changes, push to main, and Cloudflare automatically rebuilds and deploys the site. The GitOps approach is clean and gives you full version control over your content and configuration."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "The CI/CD integration is seamless once configured properly. Just make sure your build settings match your local environment to avoid deployment failures."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "## Is This Right for You? (Personal Sites vs Companies)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "Before diving into the technical details, let's be clear about when this approach works well and when it doesn't."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 49
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "**This setup is perfect for personal websites**, blogs, portfolios, and small projects. Here's why:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 52,
            "text": "**Why it's great for personal sites:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 53,
            "text": "- Zero ongoing costs (perfect when it's just for you)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 54,
            "text": "- Simple workflow once you get it set up"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 55,
            "text": "- Fast loading (your visitors will thank you)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "- You own everything (no platform can shut you down)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "- Forces you to keep things simple and focused"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 58,
            "text": "- Great for learning new skills"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "**But here's why companies shouldn't use this:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "- **No dynamic features** - no user accounts, shopping carts, forms that save to databases"
          }
        ],
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 61
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 28
          }

          {
            "lineNumber": 614
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 615,
            "text": "### Build System"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 616
          },

            "text": "#### Build Process"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 618,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 619,
            "text": "# Development"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 620,
            "text": "npm run dev                 # Start development server"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 621,
            "text": "npm run preview            # Preview production build"
          },

            "lineNumber": 623
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 625,
            "text": "npm run build              # Full production build"
          },

          },

            "lineNumber": 627,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 628
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 629,
            "text": "#### Build Pipeline"
          },

            "text": "1. **Pre-build**: Clear modules, update dependencies"

That's a critical bug where deployments might never trigger on pushes to the `master` branch.

**Hugo/Go version sprawl**: A single source of truth document or environment file is needed to manage version discrepancies, as Netlify's version is newer than others, making it unclear which version to install.

**`npm run build` always runs `update-modules`**: This process strips the go.mod require block and updates dependencies, leading to unpredictable builds. A more reliable approach would separate dependency updates from deployment builds by using a dedicated `build:ci` command that leverages vendored or committed go.sum files instead of constantly updating modules. The `clearModules` function aggressively removes the entire require block, which is then replaced by `hugo mod get -u`.

Every build resolves dependencies freshly, which introduces unpredictability.

GitHub Actions requires linux-arm64 Go for ARM runners, but it's currently missing.

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Here is a concise **review of what makes updates hard** in this repo, what was wrong in CI, and what to change next if you want a smoother maintenance loop.

---

### Critical: deploy branch vs default branch

Your Git remote default is **`master`** (`origin/HEAD` → `origin/master`), but GitHub Actions was set to deploy only on pushes to **`main`**. If you rely on that workflow for GitHub Pages, pushes to `master` would never deploy.

That is updated so the workflow runs on **`main` or `master`**, with a short comment so you can drop one branch later.

---

### Critical: wrong Go architecture on ARM runners

The workflow uses **`ubuntu-24.04-arm`** but previously downloaded **`linux-amd64`** Go. That is unreliable on ARM64. Install now picks **`arm64` vs `amd64`** from `RUNNER_ARCH`, and **`/usr/local/go/bin`** is appended to **`GITHUB_PATH`** so `hugo`/`go` resolve consistently in later steps.

---

### Hugo / Go versions are split across platforms

| Place | Hugo | Go |
|--------|------|-----|
| GitHub Actions / Amplify / GitLab | 0.141.0 | 1.23.3 |
| `netlify.toml` | **0.146.5** | **1.24.2** |
| `config/_default/module.toml` | min **0.141.0** | — |

Netlify is ahead of the rest, so “update Hugo” means editing several files and guessing which pair is canonical. **Easier updates:** pick one pinned pair, document it once (e.g. a small “Tool versions” section at the top of `README` or a single `docs/VERSIONS.md`), and mirror the same values in every host you still use.

---

### `npm run build` always upgrades Hugo modules

```8:15:/Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/package.json
    "build": "npm run update-modules && hugo --gc --minify --templateMetrics --templateMetricsHints --forceSyncStatic",
    ...
    "update-modules": "node ./scripts/clearModules.js && hugo mod clean --all && hugo mod get -u ./... && hugo mod tidy",
```

`update-modules` runs **`clearModules.js`**, which **strips the `require (...)` block from `go.mod`**, then **`hugo mod get -u`** repopulates with **upgrades** (`-u`). So every `build` is a **floating dependency refresh**, not a reproducible build from committed `go.mod` / `go.sum`.

That makes “updates” easy in the sense of always pulling latest modules, but **harder** in the sense of predictable CI, bisecting regressions, and matching local vs server. **Easier long-term:** keep `build` as plain `hugo ...` (or `hugo mod download` without `-u`), commit `go.sum`, and use a dedicated script (e.g. `npm run upgrade-modules`) when you intentionally want bumps.

---

### Many deployment paths

You have **GitHub Actions**, **GitLab CI**, **Amplify**, **Netlify**, **Vercel** (`vercel.json`), and a long README section for each. Every Hugo/Go bump touches multiple files. **Easier updates:** treat one host as primary, archive or trim configs you do not use, or add a one-line comment in each file: “Canonical versions: see README § Tool versions”.

---

### CMS vs Git

Sveltia config uses **`branch: master`**, which matches your current default branch. If you ever standardize on **`main`**, update **`static/admin/config.yml`** and the workflow together so editorial and deploy paths stay aligned.

---

### `update-theme` vs your real theme

`scripts/themeUpdate.js` pulls **`zeon-studio/hugoplate`** into **`themes/hugoplate`**, while `hugo.toml` sets **`theme = "jorap"`**. That script is easy to misread as “how I update my site theme.” Clarifying in `README` (or removing the script if unused) would reduce confusion.

---

### Summary

| Area | Issue | Direction |
|------|--------|-----------|
| GitHub Pages workflow | Wrong default branch + wrong Go on ARM | **Adjusted in repo** |
| Versions | Netlify ≠ others | Single source of truth + align hosts |
| `npm run build` | Always `-u` + clears `go.mod` requires | Split **reproducible build** vs **upgrade** |
| Hosts | Many CI/deploy files | Drop extras or document the one true pipeline |
| Scripts | `update-theme` targets Hugoplate, not `jorap` | Document or remove |

If you want a follow-up in code, the highest leverage next step is splitting **`build`** / **`upgrade-modules`** and committing a stable **`go.sum`** workflow; I can do that in a focused pass if you say which deploy target is canonical (GitHub Pages vs Netlify vs Amplify).

---

If this is the case, install Hugo directly using the distribution's package manager — for instance, in Ubuntu, run the following command:\n\nTerminal window\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nsudo apt-get install hugo\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf your package manager does not include Hugo or you would like to download a release directly, refer to the [Manual](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/framework-guides/deploy-a-hugo-site/#manual-installation) section.\n\n### Manual installation\n\nThe Hugo GitHub repository contains pre-built versions of the Hugo command-line tool for various operating systems, which can be found on [the Releases page ↗](https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/releases).\n\nFor more instruction on installing these releases, refer to [Hugo's documentation ↗](https://gohugo.io/getting-started/installing/).\n\n## Create a new project\n\nWith Hugo installed, refer to [Hugo's Quick Start ↗](https://gohugo.io/getting-started/quick-start/) to create your project or create a new project by running the &#96;hugo new&#96; command in your terminal:\n\nTerminal window\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nhugo new site my-hugo-site\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nHugo sites use themes to customize the look and feel of the statically built HTML site. There are a number of themes available at [themes.gohugo.io ↗](https://themes.gohugo.io/) — for now, use the [Ananke theme ↗](https://themes.gohugo.io/themes/gohugo-theme-ananke/) by running the following commands in your terminal:\n\nTerminal window\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\ncd my-hugo-site\n\ngit init\n\ngit submodule add https://github.com/theNewDynamic/gohugo-theme-ananke.git themes/ananke\n\necho \"theme = 'ananke'\" \u003e\u003e hugo.toml\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Create a post\n\nCreate a new post to give your Hugo site some initial content. Run the &#96;hugo new&#96; command in your terminal to generate a new post:\n\nTerminal window\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nhugo new content posts/hello-world.md\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nInside of &#96;hello-world.md&#96;, add some initial content to create your post. Remove the &#96;draft&#96; line in your post's frontmatter when you are ready to publish the post. Any posts with &#96;draft: true&#96; set will be skipped by Hugo's build process.\n\n## Create a GitHub repository\n\nCreate a new GitHub repository by visiting [repo.new ↗](https://repo.new/). After creating a new repository, go to your newly created project directory to prepare and push your local application to GitHub by running the following commands in your terminal:\n\nTerminal window\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\ngit remote add origin https://github.com/\u003cyour-gh-username\u003e/\u003crepository-name\u003e\n\ngit branch -M main\n\ngit push -u origin main\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Deploy with Cloudflare Pages\n\nTo deploy your site to Pages:\n\n1. In the Cloudflare dashboard, go to the Workers \u0026 Pages page.\n[ Go to Workers \u0026 Pages ](https://dash.cloudflare.com/?to=/:account/workers-and-pages)\n2. Select Create application.\n3. Select the Pages tab.\n4. Select Import an existing Git repository.\n5. Select the new GitHub repository that you created and then select Begin setup.\n6. In the Set up builds and deployments section, provide the following information:\n\n| Configuration option | Value | | Production branch | main |\n| -------------------- | ------ | | ----------------- | ---- |\n| Build command | hugo | | | |\n| Build directory | public | | | |\n\nWhile &#96;public&#96; is the default build directory for Hugo sites, this setting can be configured with the [publishDir setting ↗](https://gohugo.io/configuration/all/#publishdir).\n\nBase URL configuration\n\nHugo allows you to configure the &#96;baseURL&#96; of your application. This allows you to utilize the &#96;absURL&#96; helper to construct full canonical URLs. In order to do this with Pages, you must provide the &#96;-b&#96; or &#96;--baseURL&#96; flags with the &#96;CF_PAGES_URL&#96; environment variable to your &#96;hugo&#96; build command.\n\nYour final build command may look like this:\n\nTerminal window\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nhugo -b $CF_PAGES_URL\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nAfter completing deployment configuration, select the Save and Deploy. You should see Cloudflare Pages installing &#96;hugo&#96; and your project dependencies, and building your site, before deploying it.\n\nNote\n\nFor the complete guide to deploying your first site to Cloudflare Pages, refer to the [Get started guide](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/get-started/).\n\nAfter deploying your site, you will receive a unique subdomain for your project on &#96;*.pages.dev&#96;. Every time you commit new code to your Hugo site, Cloudflare Pages will automatically rebuild your project and deploy it. You will also get access to [preview deployments](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/configuration/preview-deployments/) on new pull requests, so you can preview how changes look to your site before deploying them to production.\n\n## Use a specific or newer Hugo version\n\nTo use a [specific or newer version of Hugo ↗](https://github.com/gohugoio/hugo/releases), create the &#96;HUGO_VERSION&#96; environment variable in your Pages project \u003e Settings \u003e Environment variables. Set the value as the Hugo version you want to specify (see the [Prerequisites ↗](https://gohugo.io/getting-started/quick-start/#prerequisites) for the minimum recommended version).\n\nFor example, &#96;HUGO_VERSION&#96;: &#96;0.128.0&#96;.\n\nNote\n\nIf you plan to use [preview deployments](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/configuration/preview-deployments/), make sure you also add environment variables to your Preview environment.\n\n## Learn more\n\nBy completing this guide, you have successfully deployed your Hugo site to Cloudflare Pages. For projects not listed here, consider reading the tool's documentation or framework, and submit a pull request to add it here.\n\nBuild directories indicates where your project's build command outputs the built version of your Cloudflare Pages site. Often, this defaults to the industry-standard &#96;public&#96;, but you may find that you need to customize it.\n\nUnderstanding your build configuration\n\nThe build command is provided by your framework. For example, the Gatsby framework uses &#96;gatsby build&#96; as its build command. When you are working without a framework, leave the Build command field blank. Pages determines whether a build has succeeded or failed by reading the exit code returned from the user supplied build command. Any non-zero return code will cause a build to be marked as failed. An exit code of 0 will cause the Pages build to be marked as successful and assets will be uploaded regardless of if error logs are written to standard error.\n\nThe build directory is generated from the build command. Each framework has its own naming convention, for example, the build output directory is named &#96;/public&#96; for many frameworks.\n\nThe root directory is where your site’s content lives. If not specified, Cloudflare assumes that your linked git repository is the root directory. The root directory needs to be specified in cases like monorepos, where there may be multiple projects in one repository.\n\n## Framework presets\n\nCloudflare maintains a list of build configurations for popular frameworks and tools. These are accessible during project creation. Below are some standard build commands and directories for popular frameworks and tools.\n\nIf you are not using a preset, use &#96;exit 0&#96; as your Build command.\n\n| Framework/tool | Build command | Build directory |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| React (Vite) | npm run build | dist |\n| Gatsby | npx gatsby build | public |\n| Next.js | npx @cloudflare/next-on-pages@1 | .vercel/output/static |\n| Next.js (Static HTML Export) | npx next build | out |\n| Nuxt.js | npm run build | dist |\n| Qwik | npm run build | dist |\n| Remix | npm run build | build/client |\n| Svelte | npm run build | public |\n| SvelteKit | npm run build | .svelte-kit/cloudflare |\n| Vue | npm run build | dist |\n| Analog | npm run build | dist/analog/public |\n| Astro | npm run build | dist |\n| Angular | npm run build | dist/cloudflare |\n| Brunch | npx brunch build --production | public |\n| Docusaurus | npm run build | build |\n| Elder.js | npm run build | public |\n| Eleventy | npx @11ty/eleventy | _site |\n| Ember.js | npx ember-cli build | dist |\n| GitBook | npx gitbook-cli build | _book |\n| Gridsome | npx gridsome build | dist |\n| Hugo | hugo | public |\n| Jekyll | jekyll build | _site |\n| MkDocs | mkdocs build | site |\n| Pelican | pelican content | output |\n| React Static | react-static build | dist |\n| Slate | ./deploy.sh | build |\n| Umi | npx umi build | dist |\n| VitePress | npx vitepress build | .vitepress/dist |\n| Zola | zola build | public |\n\n## Environment variables\n\nIf your project makes use of environment variables to build your site, you can provide custom environment variables:\n\n1. In the Cloudflare dashboard, go to the Workers \u0026 Pages page.\n[ Go to Workers \u0026 Pages ](https://dash.cloudflare.com/?to=/:account/workers-and-pages)\n2. Select your Pages project.\n3. Select Settings \u003e Environment variables.\n\nThe following system environment variables are injected by default (but can be overridden):\n\n| Environment Variable | Injected value | Example use-case |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| CI | true | Changing build behaviour when run on CI versus locally |\n| CF_PAGES | 1 | Changing build behaviour when run on Pages versus locally |\n| CF_PAGES_COMMIT_SHA | | Passing current commit ID to error reporting, for example, Sentry |\n| CF_PAGES_BRANCH | | Customizing build based on branch, for example, disabling debug logging on production |\n| CF_PAGES_URL | | Allowing build tools to know the URL the page will be deployed at |\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\"@context\":\"https://schema.org\",\"@type\":\"BreadcrumbList\",\"itemListElement\":[{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":1,\"item\":{\"@id\":\"/directory/\",\"name\":\"Directory\"}},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":2,\"item\":{\"@id\":\"/pages/\",\"name\":\"Pages\"}},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":3,\"item\":{\"@id\":\"/pages/configuration/\",\"name\":\"Configuration\"}},{\"@type\":\"ListItem\",\"position\":4,\"item\":{\"@id\":\"/pages/configuration/build-configuration/\",\"name\":\"Build configuration\"}}]}\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;",

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Redirects at the project root may cause Netlify to read from the site root before building.

From Netlify docs: "A _redirects file in the publish directory" - so it needs to be in public. Hugo copies static/ to public. Root _redirects wouldn't be in public unless there's a mount. This might be a pre-existing quirk - not my task to fix unless I notice. User's netlify might use build plugin to copy - ignore.

Implement:
1. `scripts/cf-pages-build.sh` - bash script for CF
2. `wrangler.toml` - with comment to set name; pages_build_output_dir
3. `package.json` - add build:cf script
4. `netlify.toml` - align versions to 0.141.0 and 1.23.3 (match majority)

README reordered deployment section to add Cloudflare Pages as option 1, including a full table with build command, bash script, output directory, and environment variables.

Cloudflare Pages supports `_redirects` in static output with the same format as Netlify. If `_redirects` is only at the repository root, users should copy it to the static directorythis is out of scope.

For Cloudflare Pages environment variables, GO_VERSION is required for Hugo modules. The README lists HUGO_VERSION, GO_VERSION, and NODE_VERSION as necessary environment variables.

Node version should be 20 or 22 LTS, matching package settings.

Node version 22 is recommended if not specified in json. Moving `_redirects` to `static/_redirects` ensures both Cloudflare and Netlify can access it, though Netlify may not use root `_redirects` if `static/_redirects` exists. This approach avoids duplication and aligns with Hugo's behavior.

Root _redirects may not work with Netlify since the publish directory is set to `public`, and root files are not deployed there. To ensure redirects function correctly, `static/_redirects` will be added with the same content, allowing Hugo to place it in the `public` directory where Netlify can access it. This avoids potential issues with broken redirects or reliance on alternative mechanisms.

I will delete the root `_redirects` file to prevent duplication or confusion, as the common pattern for Hugo is to use `static/_redirects`. Ill create `static/_redirects` with the same content and confirm that Hugo copies it correctly to `public/_redirects` during the build process. This ensures both Cloudflare Pages and Netlify can access the redirects without conflicts.</details></think>

Here is what changed for **Cloudflare Pages first, Netlify second**, and how to use it.

### Added
- **`scripts/cf-pages-build.sh`** — Pages build entrypoint: `project-setup` → `update-modules` → `hugo … -b "${CF_PAGES_URL:-/}"` so previews and `*.pages.dev` get correct base URLs (per [Cloudflare’s Hugo guide](https://developers.cloudflare.com/pages/framework-guides/deploy-a-hugo-site/)).
- **`wrangler.toml`** — `pages_build_output_dir = "public"` and a placeholder `name` (`jorap-com`); change `name` to match your Pages project in the dashboard.
- **`package.json`** — `"build:cf": "bash scripts/cf-pages-build.sh"` for local checks.

### Netlify (`netlify.toml`)
- **Hugo / Go** set to **0.141.0 / 1.23.3** so they match Cloudflare env vars, `amplify.yml`, and GitHub/GitLab CI.
- **`NODE_VERSION = "22"`** so the Node toolchain is explicit.
- **Build command** switched to **`npm run project-setup && npm run build`** (same idea as Pages; no `yarn` requirement).

### Redirects
- **`_redirects`** moved from repo root to **`static/_redirects`** so Hugo copies it into **`public/_redirects`** for both **Cloudflare Pages** and **Netlify** (root `_redirects` was never inside `public`, so it likely never applied to published builds).

### README
- Deployment section rewritten: **Cloudflare Pages → Netlify → other hosts** (Amplify, Vercel, GitHub Pages, GitLab).
- Tables for CF dashboard (**build command** `bash scripts/cf-pages-build.sh`, **output** `public`, env `HUGO_VERSION`, `GO_VERSION`, `NODE_VERSION`).
- Production environment line updated to mention **Cloudflare Pages** first.

### What you do in Cloudflare
1. Create/import a Pages project from this repo.  
2. **Build command:** `bash scripts/cf-pages-build.sh`  
3. **Build output directory:** `public`  
4. Set **`HUGO_VERSION`**, **`GO_VERSION`**, **`NODE_VERSION`** as in the README (Production + Preview).  
5. Align **`wrangler.toml`** `name` with the project slug if you use that file with Git integration.

Netlify: connect the repo and rely on **`netlify.toml`** for the rest.

If you use CMS OAuth on Pages, keep using **`TROUBLESHOOTING.md`** for `GITHUB_CLIENT_ID` / `GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET` on top of the Hugo/Go/Node vars.

---

Access via &#96;{{ .Site.Language.Locale }}&#96;[1]\n- **Name**: Returns the language tag as defined by RFC 5646 (the lowercased key from the language definition, e.g., &#96;de&#96;)[1]\n- **Direction**: Returns the text direction (&#96;ltr&#96; or &#96;rtl&#96;)[1]\n- **Label**: Returns the language name, typically used in language switchers[1]\n- **IsDefault**: Reports whether this is the default language[1]\n\n## Configuration\n\nLanguages are configured under the &#96;languages&#96; key in your config file, with properties like &#96;locale&#96;, &#96;direction&#96;, &#96;label&#96;, and &#96;weight&#96;.[2] For example:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\nlanguages:\n  de:\n    locale: de-DE\n    direction: ltr\n    label: Deutsch\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Template Usage\n\nA common use case is setting HTML attributes:[1]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;html\n\u003chtml\n  lang=\"{{ .Site.Language.Locale }}\" \n  dir=\"{{ or .Site.Language.Direction &#96;ltr&#96; }}\"\n\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can also access the Language object from pages using &#96;{{ .Language }}&#96;.[3]",

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Interesting - Hugo 0.161 flipped back:

Hugo 0.161 reverted changes where `languageCode` in config is now deprecated in favor of `locale`, and `.Language.LanguageCode` is deprecated in favor of `.Language.Locale`. 

In version 0.147, `Locale` didn't exist on the template lang wrapper, but was added in 0.158+. Cloudflare was using 0.147.7, which had the `LanguageCode()` method but lacked the `Locale` field in templates, causing errors.

For compatibility across versions:
- Use `LanguageCode()` for 0.147
- Use `Locale` for 0.158+
- In 0.161, `Locale` is correct and `LanguageCode` is deprecated

To ensure cross-version compatibility, either use conditional logic or stick to common fields.

The user on Cloudflare is on Hugo 0.147, where `LanguageCode` works, while the local user is on 0.161, which deprecates `LanguageCode` in favor of `Locale`. For cross-version compatibility, use `site.Language.Lang` or `site.Lang`, as these avoid deprecation warnings and work in both versions. While `.Site.Language.Lang` returns "en", for en-US precision, use `LanguageCode` in 0.147 and `Locale` in 0.158+. Hugo does not support easy version checks in templates, so conditional logic or consistent field usage is preferred. Using `index` reflection is not recommended.

Pinning HUGO_VERSION to 0.161+ allows using `Locale` in config and templates, reverting the `languageCode` change. Alternatively, using `site.Language.Lang` ensures compatibility across versions but may lose subtag precision. Another approach involves setting language from `site.Params` via a partial, though this requires additional configuration. The simplest solution is `site.Language.Lang | default "en"`, which avoids deprecation issues and works universally, albeit without subtag details.

The cleanest cross-version fix recommended by the Hugo community is to use `lang="{{ with site.Language }}{{ .Lang }}{{ end }}"`, which works reliably across versions. For HTML5 purposes, having `lang` as "en" is acceptable even without subtags.

Reverting `languages.toml` to use `locale` aligns with Hugo 0.161's deprecation of `languageCode`. However, since `Locale` isn't available in 0.147, a catch-22 situation arises. To resolve this, pinning `HUGO_VERSION=0.161` on Cloudflare ensures compatibility with the newer `Locale` field.</details></think>

Use `lang="{{ site.Language.Lang }}"` in `baseof.html` for universal compatibility across Hugo versions. This approach ensures the language attribute works correctly while keeping the configuration aligned with newer Hugo practices. The `locale` field in `languages.toml` remains unused for the `lang` attribute but can still be useful for other Hugo features.

To ensure compatibility and avoid version-specific issues, use `site.Language.Lang` in templates, as it works across all relevant Hugo versions and provides the language code needed for the HTML `lang` attribute. For example, `lang="{{ site.Language.Lang }}"` outputs "en", which is sufficient for most cases and aligns with RFC 5646 standards where case is insignificant. Alternatively, a fallback like `lang="{{ site.Language.Lang | default "en" }}"` can be used to ensure a default value is always present. If full locale support is required, consider setting `html_lang` in `params` and using `lang="{{ site.Params.html_lang | default site.Language.Lang }}"`, though this duplicates configuration. For simplicity and broad compatibility, sticking with `site.Language.Lang` is recommended.

The user's error stemmed from using `Locale` in `baseof.html` with Hugo 0.147, where the `Locale()` method wasn't available. Since `languageCode` was deprecated in 0.161 and `Locale()` now reads from `LanguageConfig.Locale`, the fix is to use `site.Language.Lang` in templates for compatibility across versions. Alternatively, if `languageCode` is used in config, it should be replaced with `locale` to align with 0.161+ practices, as `LanguageCode()` internally uses `Locale()` which reads from `LanguageConfig.Locale`. The original `baseof.html` used `Locale`, but `Lang` works universally and avoids version-specific issues.</details></think>
